headline: Ukraine CONFIRMED at 50.0 Functional — first post-ceasefire cycle. Ukraine's 72-hour conduct record across May 9-11 remained clean: no Ukrainian-attributed breach across any of the three trilateral ceasefire days; 1,000-prisoner list submitted on day 3. Post-ceasefire (May 11-12): Ukraine conducted defensive counterstrikes against Russian command posts and logistics in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia (within attribution-defensible defensive scope; no civilian-targeting evidence). The +3.1 dimensional carry-forward pressure (ACT +4.0; INT +3.5) from May 11 is REAFFIRMED and remains LIVE in the dimensional record per Methodology v1.2 Day-3 Sustained-Conduct Dimensional-Credit-Under-Sub-Threshold-Composite Rule. The post-ceasefire phase does not introduce new negative signal that would offset the carry-forward pressure. Confirmed at 50.0; reconstructed dimensional pressure remains 53.1; delta 3.1 sub-threshold; band Functional unchanged.

internal_reconstructed_dimensions_with_c.carry_forward_disposition: Per Methodology v1.2 Day-3 Sustained-Conduct Rule, the +3.1 dimensional credit is preserved as documented dimensional pressure rather than applied as a material composite upgrade. Maintained from May 11 confirmation; reaffirmed this cycle.

carry_forward_pressure_assessment.source_event: 72-hour US-brokered trilateral ceasefire (May 9-11). Ukraine's record: no Ukrainian-attributed breach across all 3 days; 1,000-prisoner list submitted on day 3; exchange non-completion attributable to Russian non-readiness and coordination breakdown rather than Ukrainian conduct failure.
carry_forward_pressure_assessment.post_ceasefire_phase_signal: May 11-12: Russia resumed mass offensive (200+ drones overnight). Ukraine's reported counterstrikes targeted Russian command posts and logistics in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia. No civilian-targeting evidence reported. Counterstrikes fall within attribution-defensible defensive scope. Post-ceasefire phase does NOT introduce new negative signal that would offset the carry-forward pressure.

dimensional_decomposition.ACT: +4.0 from 3.5 published (carry-forward). 3-day breach-free conduct + good-faith preparation for prisoner exchange (1,000-name list submission) demonstrate substantive responsiveness within constrained conflict context. AC2 proportionality and AC4 resource mobilization both pressured upward.
dimensional_decomposition.INT: +3.5 from 3.0 published (carry-forward). Sustained good-faith engagement with ceasefire framework under documented adversary bad-faith conditions = I1 consistency-under-pressure pressured upward. The counterparty bad-faith documentation (Russia strategic-format-exploitation + post-format-offensive-surge) provides asymmetric INT credit context.

carry_forward_pressure_assessment.accumulation_pathway: If a future cycle adds a new positive signal (e.g., prisoner exchange completion, new ceasefire structure with sustained Ukrainian compliance, post-conflict reform), the cumulative dimensional pressure could combine with new evidence to cross the 5.0pt materiality threshold. Carry-forward visible to future assessors per v1.2.

key_evidence: May 11-12 (in-window): Russia fired 200+ drones at Ukraine as truce expired (Al Jazeera). Ukraine was the recipient of the post-format surge, not the initiator.

May 12 (in-window): ISW assessment documents Ukrainian counterstrikes on Russian command posts and logistics in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia. Defensive-scope attribution; no civilian-targeting evidence.
May 12 (in-window): Multi-source reporting on post-ceasefire phase confirms asymmetric breach attribution (Russia surge; Ukraine defensive response).

Carry-forward pressure: 3-day breach-free conduct (May 9-11) + 1,000-prisoner list submission (May 11) confirmed at May 11 close. ACT +4.0; INT +3.5; reconstructed 53.1 vs published 50.0; delta 3.1 sub-threshold per v1.2.
Prisoner exchange non-completion: attributable to Russian non-readiness and coordination breakdown, not Ukrainian conduct failure. List-submission record retained as partial ACT credit.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Zelenskyy says Russia fired over 200 drones at Ukraine as truce expires
evidence.finding: Ukraine is the recipient of post-format offensive surge. Defensive-scope counterstrikes on Russian command posts and logistics. No civilian-targeting evidence.

evidence.source: ISW — Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment May 12 2026
evidence.finding: Ukrainian counterstrikes on Russian command posts and logistics in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia. Documented as defensive-scope.

evidence.source: PBS Newshour — Russia and Ukraine trade blame for continued fighting (May 11)
evidence.finding: Day-3 ceasefire close: civilian casualty attribution to Russia. Ukraine sustained 3-day breach-free record.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3 unchanged. No new AWR-relevant evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3 unchanged. No new EMP-relevant evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 unchanged at published level. Carry-forward dimensional pressure of +0.5 (to 4.0 internal) maintained per v1.2.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 unchanged. No new EQU-relevant evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3 unchanged. No new BND-relevant evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3 unchanged. No new ACC-relevant evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3 unchanged. No new SYS-relevant evidence in window.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3 unchanged at published level. Carry-forward dimensional pressure of +0.5 (to 3.5 internal) maintained per v1.2.

math_check.validator_check: Confirmation at 50.0 Functional. Carry-forward dimensional credit (+3.1) preserved per v1.2 Day-3 Sustained-Conduct Rule.

negativeSignals: Post-ceasefire phase: Russia's mass offensive resumption creates ongoing structural harm context that Ukraine must respond to. The conflict environment itself remains a structural drag on EQU and BND.
positiveSignals: 3-day breach-free record (May 9-11) sustained; 1,000-prisoner list submitted (May 11); post-ceasefire counterstrikes within defensive-scope attribution. Asymmetric breach attribution preserved.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. Ukraine's 50.0 Functional placement holds against accumulating evidence. Carry-forward dimensional pressure visible for future assessors. Counterparty (Russia) at floor with full bad-faith ceasefire-format cycle documented.
open_actions_resolved: Carry-forward dimensional credit reaffirmed per v1.2
open_actions_carried: Watch: prisoner exchange completion or further non-execution
Watch: any subsequent ceasefire structure — Ukraine's good-faith engagement record will accumulate further
methodology_consistency_check: v1.2 Day-3 Sustained-Conduct Dimensional-Credit-Under-Sub-Threshold-Composite Rule applied. Carry-forward dimensional credit maintained from May 11. No change to published composite. Methodologically consistent.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
